title: Methods and systems for proactively monitoring crude quality assurance
abstract: Methods and systems to monitor and control crude oil processing with regards to crude oil specifications for bottom sediment & water (BS&W) and salt are provided. Crude oil processing may be monitored and controlled using key performance indicators (KPIs) that include the desalter voltage and dehydrator voltage as indicators of the BS&W and salt. In response to monitoring of the desalter voltage and dehydrator voltage, a high pressure production trap (HPPT) efficiency and a dehydrator separation efficiency may be controlled via adjustment of a wash water percentage, a demulsifier dosage, a dehydrator interface level, a desalter interface level, and a differential pressure across a mixing valve.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of processing crude oil, comprising: obtaining a voltage associated with a desalter of a crude oil processing system; obtaining a voltage associated with a dehydrator of a crude oil processing system, wherein an output stream from the dehydrator is provided to the desalter; evaluating at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage as an indicator of the salt concentration in crude oil; and adjusting at least one of a plurality of control parameters in response to the evaluation to modify at least one of a separation efficiency of a high pressure production trap (HPPT) of the crude oil processing system and a separation efficiency of the dehydrator, the plurality of control parameters comprising a wash water parameter, a demulsifier parameter, an interface level of the dehydrator, an interface level of the desalter, and a mixing valve parameter. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein evaluating at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage comprises comparing at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage to a voltage threshold. 3. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein evaluating at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage comprises comparing at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage to a first operating voltage range, a second operating voltage range, and a third operating voltage range. 4. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the wash water parameter comprises a ratio of a wash water flowrate to a dry crude oil flowrate, wherein the dry crude oil flowrate comprises a flowrate of crude oil downstream from the desalter. 5. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the dehydrator output stream is mixed with a demulsifier before being provided to the desalter, wherein the demulsifier parameter comprises a ratio of a demulsifier flowrate to a sum of a dry crude oil flowrate and a produced water flowrate minus a wash water flowrate, wherein the dry crude oil flowrate comprises a flowrate of crude oil downstream from the desalter and the produced water flowrate comprises a flowrate of separated water from the dehydrator and the HPPT after the separated water is provided to a water-oil separation unit. 6. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the mixing valve parameter comprises a differential pressure across a mixing value positioned to mix wash water, a demulsifier, and the dehydrator output stream. 7. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the separation efficiency of the high pressure production trap (HPPT) comprises the ratio of HPPT water outlet flowrate to a difference between a produced water flowrate and a wash water flowrate, wherein the produced water flowrate comprises a flowrate of separated water from the dehydrator and the HPPT after the separated water is provided to a water-oil separation unit. 8. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the separation efficiency of the dehydrator comprises the ratio of dehydrator water outlet flowrate to a difference between a produced water flowrate and a wash water flowrate, wherein the produced water flowrate comprises a flowrate of separated water from the dehydrator and the HPPT after the separated water is provided to a water-oil separation unit. 9. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein adjusting at least one of the plurality of control parameters comprises adjusting at least one of the plurality of control parameters in response to the temperature of a crude oil feed to the crude oil processing facility. 10. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein adjusting the at least one of a plurality of control parameters comprises adjusting at least one of the plurality of control parameters until the adjusted control parameter is within an operating range associated with the adjusted control parameter. 11. A non- transitory computer-readable storage medium having executable code stored thereon for controlling the processing crude oil, the executable code comprising a set of instructions that causes a processor to perform operations comprising: obtaining a voltage associated with a desalter of a crude oil processing system; obtaining a voltage associated with a dehydrator of a crude oil processing system, wherein an output stream from the dehydrator is provided to the desalter; evaluating at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage as an indicator of the salt concentration in crude oil; and adjusting at least one of a plurality of control parameters in response to the evaluation to modify at least one of a separation efficiency of the high pressure production trap (HPPT) of the crude oil processing system and a separation efficiency of the dehydrator, 

 the plurality of control parameters comprising a wash water parameter, a demulsifier parameter, an interface level of the dehydrator, an interface level of the desalter, and a mixing valve parameter. 12. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 11, wherein evaluating at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage comprises comparing at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage to a voltage threshold. 13. The non- transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11 or 12, wherein evaluating at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage comprises comparing at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage to a first operating voltage range, a second operating voltage range, and a third operating voltage range. 14. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11, 12, or 13, wherein the wash water parameter comprises a ratio of a wash water flowrate to a dry crude oil flowrate, wherein the dry crude oil flowrate comprises a flowrate of crude oil downstream from the desalter. 15. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11, 12, 13, or 14, wherein the dehydrator output stream is mixed with a demulsifier before being provided to the desalter, wherein the dry crude oil flowrate comprises a flowrate of crude oil downstream from the desalter and the produced water flowrate comprises a flowrate of separated water from the dehydrator and the HPPT after the separated water is provided to a water-oil separation unit. 16. The non- transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11, 12, 13, 14, or 15, wherein the mixing valve parameter comprises a differential pressure across a mixing value positioned to mix wash water, a demulsifier, and the dehydrator output stream. 17. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, or 16, wherein the separation efficiency of the high pressure production trap (HPPT) comprises the ratio of HPPT water outlet flowrate to a difference between a produced water flowrate and a wash water flowrate, wherein the produced water flowrate comprises a flowrate of separated water from the dehydrator and the HPPT after the separated water is provided to a water-oil separation unit. 18. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, or 17, wherein the separation efficiency of the dehydrator comprises the ratio of dehydrator water outlet flowrate to a difference between a produced water flowrate and a wash water flowrate. 19. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, or 18, wherein adjusting at least one of the plurality of control parameters comprises adjusting at least one of the plurality of control parameters in response to the temperature of a crude oil feed to the crude oil processing facility. 20. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, or 19, wherein adjusting the at least one of a plurality of control parameters comprises adjusting at least one of the plurality of control parameters until the adjusted control parameter is within an operating range associated with the adjusted control parameter. 21. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claims 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 21, the operations comprising providing a graphical user interface on a display coupled to the processor, the graphical user interface comprising a user interface element graphically indicating the value of the parameter relative to a plurality of operational ranges. 22. The non- transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 21, wherein the user interface element comprises a first color associated with a first range of the plurality of operating ranges, a second color associated with a second range of the plurality of operating ranges, and a third color associated with a third range of the plurality of operating ranges. 23. A crude oil processing system, comprising: a desalter; a dehydrator; a high pressure production trap (HPPT); a processor; a non-transitory computer-readable memory coupled to the processor and having executable code stored thereon for controlling the processing of crude oil, the executable code comprising a set of instructions that causes the processor to perform operations comprising: obtaining a voltage associated with the desalter; obtaining a voltage associated with the dehydrator; evaluating at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage as an indicator of the salt concentration in the crude oil; and 

 adjusting at least one of a plurality of control parameters in response to the evaluation to modify at least one of a separation efficiency of the HPPT and a separation efficiency of the dehydrator, the plurality of control parameters comprising a wash water parameter, a demulsifier parameter, an interface level of the dehydrator, an interface level of the desalter, and a mixing valve parameter. 24. The system of claim 23, wherein evaluating at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage comprises comparing at least one of the first voltage and the second voltage to a first operating voltage range, a second operating voltage range, and a third operating voltage range. 25. The system of claims 23 or 24, wherein the wash water parameter comprises a ratio of a wash water flowrate to a dry crude oil flowrate, wherein the dry crude oil flowrate comprises a flowrate of crude oil downstream from the desalter. 26. The system of claims 23, 24, or 25, wherein the dehydrator output stream is mixed with a demulsifier before being provided to the desalter, wherein the dry crude oil flowrate comprises a flowrate of crude oil downstream from the desalter and the produced water flowrate comprises a flowrate of separated water from the dehydrator and the HPPT after the separated water is provided to a water-oil separation unit. 27. The system of claims 23, 24, 25, or 26, wherein the mixing valve parameter comprises a differential pressure across a mixing value positioned to mix wash water, a demulsifier, and the dehydrator output stream. 28. The system of claims 23, 24, 25, 26, or 27, wherein the separation efficiency of the high pressure production trap (HPPT) comprises the ratio of HPPT water outlet flowrate to a difference between a produced water flowrate and a wash water flowrate, wherein the produced water flowrate comprises a flowrate of separated water from the dehydrator and the HPPT after the separated water is provided to a water-oil separation unit. 29. The system of claims 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, or 28, wherein the separation efficiency of the dehydrator comprises the ratio of dehydrator water outlet flowrate to a difference between a produced water flowrate and a wash water flowrate, wherein the produced water flowrate 

 comprises a flowrate of separated water from the dehydrator and the HPPT after the separated water is provided to a water-oil separation unit.
